skill.name: data-exploration-tool
skill.description: Systematic database and table profiling for DBX Studio. Use when a user wants to understand their data, explore schema structure, or profile a dataset.
skill.summary: Systematic database profiling skill for DBX Studio that helps users understand their data structure, discover table relationships, and assess data quality.
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: Security review complete. Static analyzer flagged 19 potential issues (backticks, crypto keywords, reconnaissance patterns), but manual review confirms all are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation with SQL query examples - no actual shell commands, cryptographic code, or reconnaissance tools. This is a legitimate database exploration skill with no security concerns.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected backtick characters as shell execution. Review confirms these are markdown inline code fences (e.g., `read_schema`), not actual shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown are standard code formatting, not shell execution. No actual shell commands or code execution present in file.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'DESC' in SQL ORDER BY clause as weak crypto. Review confirms this is standard SQL descending sort, not encryption.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: ORDER BY 2 DESC is basic SQL syntax. The analyzer misinterprets 'DESC' as a cryptographic algorithm, but context confirms it's SQL sorting.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged SQL join pattern notation as reconnaissance. Review confirms this is standard database terminology for table relationships.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The notation 'users.id → orders.user_id' describes SQL foreign key relationships, not system reconnaissance. Standard database documentation.

content.user_title: Explore Database Schemas and Profile Data
content.value_statement: This skill helps users systematically explore database structures, understand table relationships, and assess data quality through a structured profiling workflow.
actual_capabilities: List all tables in a schema using read_schema tool
Describe individual table structures with column details using describe_table
Gather table statistics including row counts and data distributions
Identify potential foreign key relationships through column naming patterns
Calculate data quality metrics like null rates and completeness scores
Generate exploration queries for row counts, distinct values, and date ranges
limitations: Only works within DBX Studio environment with access to database tools
Does not modify data - read-only exploration only
Quality scoring thresholds are fixed and may not fit all use cases
Foreign key detection is pattern-based and may miss non-standard relationships

use_cases.title: New Database Onboarding
use_cases.description: Quickly understand the structure of a new database by exploring schemas, tables, and their relationships before writing queries.
use_cases.target_user: Developers joining a new project or analysts exploring a new data source

use_cases.title: Data Quality Audit
use_cases.description: Assess data completeness by checking null rates, identifying empty columns, and reviewing data distributions across tables.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers and QA specialists performing data health checks

use_cases.title: Query Writing Preparation
use_cases.description: Map out table relationships and understand column types before writing complex joins or aggregations.
use_cases.target_user: SQL developers and analysts preparing data extraction queries

prompt_templates.title: Basic Schema Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-exploration skill to explore the schema and tell me what tables are available in the public schema.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see what tables exist in their database

prompt_templates.title: Table Structure Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-exploration skill to profile the users table. Include column names, types, and null rates.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants detailed information about a specific table

prompt_templates.title: Relationship Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-exploration skill to discover how the orders table relates to other tables. Look for foreign key patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to understand table connections for joining data

prompt_templates.title: Data Quality Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-exploration skill to run a quality check on all tables in the public schema. Identify tables with high null rates or poor completeness.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a comprehensive data health report

output: Found 5 tables: users, orders, products, order_items, categories
Users table: 1,250 rows, 8 columns (id, email, name, created_at, etc.)
Orders table: 5,432 rows, references users.id via user_id
Data quality: users table is 98% complete, orders table is 95% complete
Columns: id (int), user_id (int FK→users), total (decimal), status (varchar), created_at (timestamp)

best_practices: Start with read_schema to get a complete table inventory before diving into details
Use the three-phase workflow (Discovery → Profiling → Relationships) for consistent results
Check null rates early to identify incomplete data before building queries on those columns
anti_patterns: Jumping straight to writing queries without first exploring the schema structure
Assuming column names are intuitive without verifying with describe_table
Ignoring null rate information when building aggregations or joins

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses DBX Studio tools: read_schema, describe_table, get_table_stats, and get_table_data for exploration.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my data?
faq.answer: No, this is a read-only skill. It only reads schema information and sample data for exploration purposes.

faq.question: How does the quality scoring work?
faq.answer: Quality scores are based on null rates: Green (>95% filled), Yellow (80-95%), Orange (50-80%), Red (<50%).

faq.question: Does this work with any database type?
faq.answer: This skill works with any database supported by DBX Studio that has the required exploration tools available.

faq.question: Can I use this to find foreign keys?
faq.answer: The skill identifies potential foreign keys column naming patterns (* through_id), but cannot detect all explicit foreign key constraints.

faq.question: What schema should I explore first?
faq.answer: Start with the 'public' schema for typical databases. Use read_schema to see all available schemas first.
